# IfcTypeConstraint.CanBeMet method

Returns false if the constraint has no allowed types, meaning that it represents an impossible condition where no entity can satisfy the constraint. A null constraint is not considered impossible, as it represents an unconstrained set of types rather than an empty set.

```csharp
public static bool CanBeMet(this IIfcTypeConstraint? constraint)
```
